conversations.content: Let's do a special rule knowledge quiz to test your understanding of special rules.

Answer using the special rule explained earlier. A student at Star Middle School has been absent for three consecutive days without excuse. According to the student attendance management regulations, how should this be handled?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>How to handle a student being absent for three consecutive days without excuse</content>

Relevant memory fragment: [mem-id:80001] Time: 2026-06-08-18:00, Content: Star Middle School Student Attendance Management Regulations: If a student is late without excuse once, the homeroom teacher gives a verbal warning and records a deduction of 1 point; if a student is late without excuse three times, issue a public reprimand and request a parent-school meeting; if a student misses one class without excuse, record a demerit and cancel eligibility for monthly awards; if a student is absent for three consecutive days without excuse, impose probation and report to the education bureau for filing. Low-relevance memory fragments: none.

conversations.think: Memory [mem-id:80001] stipulates that if a student is absent for three consecutive days without excuse, impose probation and report to the education bureau for filing.
conversations.content: According to the Star Middle School Student Attendance Management Regulations, if a student is absent for three consecutive days without excuse, they must be given probation and reported to the education bureau for filing!
Correct! You have mastered this rule.
